flashcards.definition: The first solution returned by an inverse trig function within its restricted range.
If sin θ = a, then θ = arcsin(a) + 2nπ or θ = π − arcsin(a) + 2nπ.
If cos θ = a, then θ = ±arccos(a) + 2nπ.
If tan θ = a, then θ = arctan(a) + nπ.
Rearrange the equation so the trig function is alone on one side before applying the inverse.
